adhkar.transliteration: allahuma iiniy aasaaluka birahmatika alatiy wasi'at kula shay'in aan taghfira liy.
adhkar.instruction: (Allahumma inne as-aluka birahmatikal-latee wasiAAat kulla shay, an taghfira lee.)

adhkar.arabic: ((إِذَا أَكَلَ أَحَدُكُمْ طَعَاماً فَلْيَقُلْ بِسْمِ اللَّهِ، فَإِنْ نَسِيَ فِي أَوَّلِهِ فَلْيَقُلْ بسمِ اللَّهِ فِي أَوَّلِهِ وَآخِرِهِ)).
adhkar.translation: (When you are about to eat, you should say:(Bismil-lah.)and if you forget to say it before starting, then you should say (when you remember):In the name of Allah in it’s beginning and end.)
adhkar.transliteration: iidhaa aakala aahadukum ta'aamaan falyaqul bismi allahi faiin nasiya fiy aawalihi falyaqul bsmi allahi fiy aawalihi waaakhirihi.
adhkar.instruction: (When you are about to eat, you should say:(Bismil-lah.)and if you forget to say it before starting, then you should say (when you remember):Bismil-lahi fee awwalihi wa-akhirih.)

adhkar.arabic: ((مَنْ أَطْعَمَهُ اللَّهُ الطَّعَامَ فَلْيَقُلْ: اللَّهُمَّ بَارِكْ لَنَا فِيهِ وَأَطْعِمْنَا خَيْراً مِنْهُ، وَمَنْ سَقَاهُ اللَّهُ لَبَناً فَلْيَقُلْ اللَّهُمَّ بَارِكْ لَنَا فِيهِ وَزِدْنَا مِنْهُ)).
adhkar.translation: (Whomever Allah feeds, should say:(O Allah, bless it for us and feed us better than it.) and whomever Allah gives milk to drink should say:(O Allah, bless it for usand give us more of it.))
adhkar.transliteration: man aat'amahu allahu alta'aama falyaqul: allahuma baarik lanaa fiyhi waaat'imnaa khayraan minhu waman saqaahu allahu labanaan falyaqul allahuma baarik lanaa fiyhi wazidnaa minhu.
adhkar.instruction: (Whomever Allah feeds, should say:(Allahumma barik lana feehi wa-atAAimna khayran minh.) and whomever Allah gives milk to drink should say:(Allahumma barik lana feehi wazidna minh.))

adhkar.arabic: ((الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي أَطْعَمَنِي هَذَا، وَرَزَقَنِيهِ، مِنْ غَيْرِ حَوْلٍ مِنِّي وَلاَ قُوَّةٍ)).
adhkar.translation: (All praise is for Allah who fed me this and provided it for me without any might nor power from myself.)
adhkar.transliteration: alhamdulillahilladhi at'amani hadha wa razaqanihi min ghayri hawlin minni wa la quwwah
adhkar.instruction: (Alhamdu lillahil-lathee atAAamanee hatha warazaqaneehi min ghayri hawlin minnee wala quwwah.)

adhkar.arabic: ((الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ حَمْداً كَثِيراً طَيِّباً مُبَارَكاً فِيهِ، غَيْرَ [مَكْفِيٍّ وَلاَ ] مُوَدَّعٍ، وَلاَ مُسْتَغْنَىً عَنْهُ رَبَّنَا)).
adhkar.translation: (Allah be praised with an abundant beautiful blessed praise, a never-ending praise, a praise which we will never bid farewell to and an indispensable praise, He is our Lord.)(There are other views as regards to the understanding of this supplication, from them: Allah be praised with an abundant beautiful blessed praise. He is The One Who is sufficient, feeds and is not fed. The One Who is longed for, along with that which is with Him and The One Who is needed, He is our Lord.)
adhkar.transliteration: alhamdu lilahi hamdaan kathiyraan tayibaan mubaarakaan fiyhi ghayra makfiyin walaa muwada'in walaa mustaghnaaan 'anhu rabanaa.
adhkar.instruction: (Alhamdu lillahi hamdan katheeran tayyiban mubarakan feeh, ghayra makfiyyin wala muwaddaAAin, wala mustaghnan AAanhu rabbuna.)

adhkar.category_name: A dinner guest's invocation for his host
adhkar.arabic: ((اللَّهُمَّ بَارِكْ لَهُمْ فِيمَا رَزَقْتَهُم، وَاغْفِرْ لَهُمْ وَارْحَمْهُمْ)).
adhkar.translation: (O Allah, bless for them, that which You have provided them, forgive them and have mercy upon them.)
adhkar.transliteration: allahuma baarik lahum fiymaa razaqtahum waaghfir lahum waarhamhum.
adhkar.instruction: (Allahumma barik lahum feema razaqtahum, waghfir lahum warhamhum.)

adhkar.category_name: Invocation for someone who gives you drink or offers it to you
adhkar.arabic: ((اللَّهُمَّ أَطْعِمْ مَنْ أَطْعَمَنِي، وَاسْقِ مَنْ سَقَانِي)).
adhkar.translation: (O Allah, feed him who fed me, and provide with drink him who provided me with drink.)
adhkar.transliteration: allahuma aat'im man aat'amaniy waasqi man saqaaniy.
adhkar.instruction: (Allahumma atAAim man atAAamanee wasqi man saqanee.)

adhkar.category_name: Invocation for a family who invites you to break your fast with them
adhkar.arabic: ((أَفْطَرَ عِنْدَكُمُ الصَّائِمُونَ، وَأَكَلَ طَعَامَكُمُ الْأَبْرَارُ، وَصَلَّتْ عَلَيْكُمُ الْمَلاَئِكَةُ)).
adhkar.translation: (May the fasting break their fast in your home, and may the dutiful and pious eat your food, and may the angles send prayers upon you.)
adhkar.transliteration: aaftara 'indakumu alsaaimuwna waaakala ta'aamakumu alaabraaru wasalat 'alaykumu almalaaikahu.
adhkar.instruction: (Aftara AAindakumus-sa-imoon, wa-akala taAAamakumul-abrar, wasallat AAalaykumul-mala-ikah.)

adhkar.category_name: Invocation for someone who offers you food when you are fasting, which you decline
adhkar.arabic: ((إِذَا دُعِيَ أَحَدُكُمْ فَلْيُجِبْ، فَإِنْ كَانَ صَائِماً فَلْيُصَلِّ، وَإِنْ كَانَ مُفْطِراً فَلْيَطْعَمْ)) ، وَمَعْنَى فَلْيُصَلِّ أَيْ فَلْيَدْعُ.
adhkar.transliteration: iidhaa du'iya aahadukum falyujib faiin kaana saaimaan falyusali waiin kaana muftiraan falyat'am wama'naa falyusali aay falyad'u.
adhkar.instruction: (If you are not invited (to a meal) then answer.  If  you happen to be fasting, then supplicate (for those present) and if you are not fasting, then eat.)

adhkar.category_name: What to say when you are fasting and someone is rude to you
adhkar.arabic: ((إِنِّي صَائِمٌ، إِنِّي صَائِمٌ)).
adhkar.translation: (I am fasting, I am fasting.)
adhkar.transliteration: iiniy saaimun iiniy saaimun.
adhkar.instruction: (Innee sa-im, innee sa-im.)

adhkar.category_name: Invocation for when you see the first dates of the season
adhkar.arabic: ((اللَّهُمَّ بَارِكْ لَنَا فِي ثَمَرِنَا، وَبَارِكْ لَنَا فِي مَدِينَتِنَا، وَبَارِكْ لَنَا فِي صَاعِنَا، وَبَارِكْ لَنَا فِي مُدِّنَا)).
adhkar.translation: (O Allah, bless our fruit for us, bless our town for us, bless our saAA for us and bless our mudd for us.)(A saAA is equivalent to four mudds and a mudd is equivalent to a dry measure of an average man’s two palms.)
adhkar.transliteration: allahuma baarik lanaa fiy thamarinaa wabaarik lanaa fiy madiynatinaa wabaarik lanaa fiy saa'inaa wabaarik lanaa fiy mudinaa.
adhkar.instruction: (Allahumma barik lana fee thamarina, wabarik lana fee madeenatina, wabarik lana fee saAAina wabarik lana fee muddina.)

adhkar.arabic: ((إِذَا عَطَسَ أَحَدُكُم فَلْيَقُلِ الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ، وَلْيَقُلْ لَهُ أَخُوهُ أَوْ صَاحِبُهُ: يَرْحَمُكَ اللَّهُ، فَإِذَا قَالَ لَهُ: يَرحَمُكَ اللَّهُ، فَلْيَقُلْ: يَهْدِيكُمُ اللَّهُ وَيُصْلِحُ بَالَكُمْ)).
adhkar.translation: (When one of you sneezes he should say:(All praise if for Allah.) and his brother or companion should say to him: (May Allah have mercy upon you.) and he (i.e. the one who sneezed) replies back to him:(May Allah guide you and rectify your condition.))
adhkar.transliteration: iidhaa 'atasa aahadukum falyaquli alhamdu lilahi walyaqul lahu aakhuwhu aaw saahibuhu: yarhamuka allahu faiidhaa qaala lahu: yarhamuka allahu falyaqul: yahdiykumu allahu wayuslihu baalakum.
adhkar.instruction: (When one of you sneezes he should say:(Alhamdu lillah.) and his brother or companion should say to him: (Yarhamukal-lah.) and he (i.e. the one who sneezed) replies back to him:(Yahdeekumul-lahu wayuslihu balakum.))

adhkar.category_name: What to say to the disbeliever if he sneezes and praises Allah
adhkar.arabic: ((يَهْدِيكُمُ اللَّهُ وَيُصْلِحُ بَالَكُمْ)).
adhkar.translation: (May Allah guide you and rectify your condition.)
adhkar.transliteration: yahdikumullah wa yuslihu balakum
adhkar.instruction: (Yahdeekum wayuslihu balakum.)

adhkar.arabic: ((بَارَكَ اللَّهُ لَكَ، وَبَارَكَ عَلَيْكَ، وَجَمَعَ بَيْنَكُمَا فِي خَيْرٍ)).
adhkar.translation: (May Allah bless for you (your spouse) and bless you, and may He unite both of you in goodness.)
adhkar.transliteration: baaraka allahu laka wabaaraka 'alayka wajama'a baynakumaa fiy khayrin.
adhkar.instruction: (Barakal-lahu lak, wabaraka AAalayk, wajamaAAa baynakuma fee khayr.)

adhkar.category_name: The groom’s supplication on the wedding night or when buying an animal
adhkar.arabic: إِذَا تَزَوَّجَ أَحَدُكُمُ امْرَأَةً، أَوْ إِذَا اشْتَرَى خَادِماً فَلْيَقُلْ: ((اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ خَيْرَهَا، وَخَيْرَ مَا جَبَلْتَهَا عَلَيْهِ، وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّهَا، وَشَرِّ مَا جَبَلْتَهَا عَلَيْهِ، وَإِذَا اشْتَرَى بَعِيراً فَلْيَأْخُذْ بِذِرْوَةِ سَنَامِهِ وَلْيَقُلْ مِثْلَ ذَلِكَ)).
adhkar.translation: (when you marry a woman or buy a maidservant, you should say:(O Allah, I ask You for the goodness within her and the goodness that you have made her inclined towards, and I take refuge with You from the evil within her and the evil that you have made her inclined towards.))(and if you buy a camel, then you should take hold of it’s hump and say likewise.)
adhkar.transliteration: iidhaa tazawaja aahadukumu amraaahan aaw iidhaa ashtaraa khaadimaan falyaqul: allahuma iiniy aasaaluka khayrahaa wakhayra maa jabaltahaa 'alayhi waaa'uwdhu bika min sharihaa washari maa jabaltahaa 'alayhi waiidhaa ashtaraa ba'iyraan falyaakhudh bidhirwahi sanaamihi walyaqul mithla dha
adhkar.instruction: (when you marry a woman or buy a maidservant, you should say: (Allahumma innee as-aluka khayraha wakhayra ma jabaltaha AAalayh, wa-aAAoothu bika min sharriha washarri ma jabaltaha AAalayh.))(and if you buy a camel, then you should take hold of it’s hump and say likewise.)

adhkar.category_name: Invocation to be recited before intercourse
adhkar.arabic: ((بِسْمِ اللَّهِ، اللَّهُمَّ جَنِّبْنَا الشَّيْطَانَ، وَجَنِّبِ الشَّيْطَانَ مَا رَزَقْتَنَا)).
adhkar.translation: (In the name of Allah. O Allah, keep the devil away from us and keep the devil away from what you have blessed us with.)
adhkar.transliteration: bismi allahi allahuma janibnaa alshaytaana wajanibi alshaytaana maa razaqtanaa.
adhkar.instruction: (Bismil-lah, allahumma jannibnash-shaytan, wajannibish-shaytana ma razaqtana.)

adhkar.arabic: ((أَعُوذُ بِاللَّهِ مِنَ الشَّيْطَانِ الرَّجِيمِ)).
adhkar.translation: (I take refuge with Allah from the accursed devil.)
adhkar.transliteration: aa'uwdhu biallahi mina alshaytaani alrajiymi.
adhkar.instruction: (aAAoothu billahi minash-shaytanir-rajeem.)

adhkar.category_name: What to say if you see someone afflicted by misfortune
adhkar.arabic: ((الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي عَافَانِي مِمَّا ابْتَلاَكَ بِهِ، وَفَضَّلَنِي عَلَى كَثِيرٍ مِمَّنْ خَلَقَ تَفْضِيلاً)).
adhkar.translation: (All praise is for Allah Who saved me from that which He tested you with and Who most certainly favoured me over much of His creation.)
adhkar.transliteration: alhamdu lilahi aladhiy 'aafaaniy mimaa abtalaaka bihi wafadalaniy 'alaa kathiyrin miman khalaqa tafdiylaan.
adhkar.instruction: (Alhamdu lillahil-lathee AAafanee mimmab-talaka bih, wafaddalanee AAala katheerin mimman khalaqa tafdeela.)

adhkar.category_name: What to say while sitting in an assembly
adhkar.arabic: ((عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ رضي الله عنه قَاَلَ: كَانَ يُعَدُّ لِرَسُولِ اللَّهِ صلى الله عليه وسلم فِي الْمَجْلِسِ الوَاحِدِ مِائَةُ مَرَّةٍ مِنْ قَبْلِ أَنْ يَقُومَ: ((رَبِّ اغْفِرْ لِي، وَتُبْ عَلَيَّ، إِنَّكَ أَنْتَ التَّوَّابُ الغَفُورُ)).
adhkar.translation: (Ibn AAumar  said: It would be counted that the Messenger of Allah  would say one hundred times at any one sitting before getting up: (O my Lord, forgive me and turn towards me (to accept my repentance). Verily You are The Oft-Returning. The Oft-Forgiving.))
adhkar.transliteration: 'ani abni 'umara rdy allh 'nh qaaala: kaana yu'adu lirasuwli allahi sla allh 'lyh wslm fiy almajlisi alwaahidi miaahu marahin min qabli aan yaquwma: rabi aghfir liy watub 'alaya iinaka aanta altawaabu alghafuwru.
adhkar.instruction: (Ibn AAumar  said: It would be counted that the Messenger of Allah  would say one hundred times at any one sitting before getting up: (Rabbigh-fir lee watub AAalay, innaka antat-tawwabul-ghafoor.))

adhkar.arabic: ((سُبْحَانَكَ اللَّهُمَّ وَبِحَمْدِكَ، أَشْهَدُ أَنْ لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ أَنْتَ، أَسْتَغْفِرُكَ وَأَتُوبُ إِلَيْكَ)).
adhkar.translation: (How perfect You are O Allah, and I praise You.  I bear witness that None has the right to be worshipped except You.  I seek Your forgiveness and turn to You in repentance.)
adhkar.transliteration: subhaanaka allahuma wabihamdika aashhadu aan laa iilaha iilaa aanta aastaghfiruka waaatuwbu iilayka.
adhkar.instruction: (Subhanakal-lahumma wabihamdik, ashhadu an la ilaha illa ant, astaghfiruka wa-atoobu ilayk.)

adhkar.category_name: vocation for someone who says: May Allah forgive you
adhkar.translation: (AAabdullah Ibn Sarjis  said: ‘I went to see the Prophet  and ate from his food and then said to him: (May Allah forgive you, O Messenger of Allah.) he  replied: (and you.))
adhkar.transliteration: walaka.
adhkar.instruction: (AAabdullah Ibn Sarjis  said: ‘I went to see the Prophet  and ate from his food and then said to him:(Ghafaral-lahu laka ya rasoolal-lah.) he  replied: (wa-lak))

adhkar.category_name: Invocation for someone who does good to you
adhkar.arabic: ((جَزَاكَ اللَّهُ خَيْراً)).
adhkar.translation: (If someone does you a favour and you say: (May Allah reward you with goodness.) then you have indeed excelled in praising him.)
adhkar.transliteration: jazaaka allahu khayraan.
adhkar.instruction: (If someone does you a favour and you say: (Jazakal-lahu khayran.) then you have indeed excelled in praising him.)

adhkar.category_name: Invocation for Allah's protection from the False Messiah
adhkar.arabic: ((مَنْ حَفِظَ عَشْرَ آيَاتٍ مِنْ أَوَّلِ سُورَةِ الْكَهْفِ عُصِمَ مِنَ الدَّجَّالِ)) ، وَالْاسْتِعَاذَةُ بِاللَّهِ مِنْ فِتْنَتِهِ عَقِبَ التَّشَهُّدِ الْأَخِيرِ مِنْ كُلِّ صَلاَةٍ.
adhkar.translation: (Dajjal: among the great signs of the last hour and the greatest trials to befall mankind, which every Prophet has warned about.  Most of mankind will folow him. He will appear from Asbahan, Iran at the time when Muslims will conquer Constantinople.  He will be given special powers and will make the truth seem false and vice versa. He will claim to be righteous and then he will claim prophet-hood and finally, divinity. From his features is that he will be blind in his right eye which is a definite proof that contradicts his claim to be Allah as it is a sign of imperfection.  The word Kafir will be written between his eyes which every believer, literate or illiterate will recognise.)(Whoever memorises the first ten verses of soorat Al-Kahf will be protected from Dajjal.)(One should also seek refuge with Allah from the tribulations of the Dajjal after the last tashahhud in prayer.(Refer to supplications #55 & #56))
adhkar.transliteration: man hafiza 'ashra aayaatin min aawali suwrahi alkahfi 'usima mina aldajaali waalasti'aadhahu biallahi min fitnatihi 'aqiba altashahudi alaakhiyri min kuli salaahin.

adhkar.category_name: Invocation for someone who tells you I love you for the sake of Allah
adhkar.arabic: ((أَحَبَّكَ الَّذِي أَحْبَبْتَنِي لَهُ)).
adhkar.translation: (May He, for whom you have loved me, love you.)
adhkar.transliteration: aahabaka aladhiy aahbabtaniy lahu.
adhkar.instruction: (Ahabbakal-lathee ahbabtanee lah.)

adhkar.category_name: Invocation for someone who offers you a share of his wealth
adhkar.arabic: ((بَارَكَ اللَّهُ لَكَ فِي أَهْلِكَ وَمَالِكَ)).
adhkar.translation: (May Allah bless for you, your family and wealth.)
adhkar.transliteration: baaraka allahu laka fiy aahlika wamaalika.
adhkar.instruction: (Barakal-lahu laka fee ahlika wamalik.)

adhkar.category_name: Invocation (upon receipt of the loan) for someone who lends you money
adhkar.arabic: ((بارَكَ اللَّهُ لَكَ فِي أَهْلِكَ وَمَالِكَ، إِنَّمَا جَزَاءُ السَّلَفِ الْحَمْدُ وَالأَدَاءُ)).
adhkar.translation: (May Allah bless for you, your family and wealth. Surely commendation and payment are the reward for a loan.)
adhkar.transliteration: baraka allahu laka fiy aahlika wamaalika iinamaa jazaa'u alsalafi alhamdu waalaadaa'u.
adhkar.instruction: (Barakal-lahu laka fee ahlika wamalik, innama jaza-os-salafil-hamdu wal-ada'.)

adhkar.arabic: ((اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ أَنْ أُشْرِكَ بِكَ وَأَنَا أَعْلَمُ، وَأَسْتَغْفِرُكَ لِمَا لاَ أَعْلَمُ)).
adhkar.translation: (shirk: to associate others with Allah in those things which are specific to Him.  This can occur in (1) belief, e.g. to believe that other than Allah has the power to benefit or harm, (2) speech, e.g. to swear by other than Allah and (3) action, e.g. to bow or prostrate to other than Allah.)(O Allah, I take refuge in You lest I should commit shirk with You knowingly and I seek Your forgiveness for what I do unknowingly.)
adhkar.transliteration: allahuma iiniy aa'uwdhu bika aan aushrika bika waaanaa aa'lamu waaastaghfiruka limaa laa aa'lamu.
adhkar.instruction: (shirk: to associate others with Allah in those things which are specific to Him.  This can occur in (1) belief, e.g. to believe that other than Allah has the power to benefit or harm, (2) speech, e.g. to swear by other than Allah and (3) action, e.g. to bow or prostrate to other than Allah.)(Allahumma innee aAAoothu bika an oshrika bika wa-ana aAAlam, wa-astaghfiruka lima la aAAlam.)

adhkar.category_name: Invocation for someone who tells you: May Allah bless you
adhkar.arabic: ((وَفِيكَ بَارَكَ اللَّهُ)).
adhkar.translation: (Aaishah reported that the Messenger of Allah  was given a sheep and he ordered for it’s distribution.  When the servant would come back (from distributing it), AAaishah would ask: ‘What did they say?’, he replied: They would supplicate: (May Allah bless you all.) AAaishah would then say: (and may Allah bless them.))(we return their supplication in a similar way and our reward remains with us.)
adhkar.transliteration: wafiyka baaraka allahu.
adhkar.instruction: (Aaishah reported that the Messenger of Allah  was given a sheep and he ordered for it’s distribution.  When the servant would come back (from distributing it), AAaishah would ask: ‘What did they say?’, he replied: They would supplicate: (Barakal-lahu feekum.) AAaishah would then say: (Wafeehim barakal-lah.))(we return their supplication in a similar way and our reward remains with us.)

adhkar.arabic: ((اللَّهُمَّ لاَ طَيْرَ إِلاَّ طَيْرُكَ، وَلاَ خَيْرَ إِلاَّ خَيْرُكَ، وَلاَ إِلَهَ غَيْرُكَ)).
adhkar.translation: (This supplication is used whenever one initially thinks a casual event or occurrence to foretell good or evil, using it as a basis to determine which action he should undertake, but he then denounces such a link, relies on Allah and then says this supplication as an expiation for this act, since it falls under the category of shirk.)(O Allah, there is no omen but there is reliance on You, there is no good except Your good and none has the right to be worshipped except You.)
adhkar.transliteration: allahuma laa tayra iilaa tayruka walaa khayra iilaa khayruka walaa iilaha ghayruka.
adhkar.instruction: (This supplication is used whenever one initially thinks a casual event or occurrence to foretell good or evil, using it as a basis to determine which action he should undertake, but he then denounces such a link, relies on Allah and then says this supplication as an expiation for this act, since it falls under the category of shirk.)(Allahumma la tayra illa tayruk, wala khayra illa khayruk, wala ilaha ghayruk.)

adhkar.category_name: Invocation for riding in a vehicle or on an animal
adhkar.arabic: ((بِسْمِ اللَّهِ، وَالْحَمْدُ للَّهِ ﴿سُبْحَانَ الَّذِي سَخَّرَ لَنَا هَذَا وَمَا كُنَّا لَهُ مُقْرِنِينَ، وَإِنَّا إِلَى رَبِّنَا لَمُنقَلِبُونَ﴾، ((الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ، الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ، الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ، اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، سُبْحَانَكَ اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي ظَلَمْتُ نَفْسِي فَاغْفِرْ لِي؛ فَإِنَّهُ لاَ يَغْفِرُ الذُّنُوبَ إِلاَّ أَنْتَ)).
adhkar.translation: (In the name of Allah and all praise is for Allah.  How perfect He is, the One Who has placed this (transport) at our service and we ourselves would not have been capable of that, and to our Lord is our final destiny.  All praise is for Allah, All praise is for Allah, All praise is for Allah,  Allah is the greatest, Allah is the greatest, Allah is the greatest. How perfect You are, O Allah, verily I have wronged my soul, so forgive me, for surely none can forgive sins except You.)
adhkar.transliteration: bismi allahi waalhamdu llahi subhaana aladhiy sakhara lanaa hadhaa wamaa kunaa lahu muqriniyna waiinaa iilaa rabinaa lamunqalibuwna alhamdu lilahi alhamdu lilahi alhamdu lilahi allahu aakbaru allahu aakbaru allahu aakbaru subhaanaka allahuma iiniy zalamtu
adhkar.instruction: (Bismil-lah, walhamdu lillah, subhanal-lathee sakhkhara lana hatha wama kunna lahu muqrineen, wainna ila rabbina lamunqaliboon, alhamdu lillah, alhamdu lillah, alhamdu lillah, Allahu akbar, Allahu akbar, Allahu akbar, subhanakal-lahumma innee thalamtu nafsee faghfir lee fainnahu la yaghfiruth-thunooba illa ant.)

adhkar.arabic: اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، ﴿سُبْحَانَ الَّذِي سَخَّرَ لَنَا هَذَا وَمَا كُنَّا لَهُ مُقْرِنِينَ * وَإِنَّا إِلَى رَبِّنَا لَمُنقَلِبُونَ﴾ ((اللَّهُمَّ إِنّا نَسْأَلُكَ فِي سَفَرِنَا هَذَا البِرَّ وَالتَّقْوَى، وَمِنَ الْعَمَلِ مَا تَرْضَى، اللَّهُمَّ هَوِّنْ عَلَيْنَا سَفَرَنَا هَذَا وَاطْوِ عَنَّا بُعْدَهُ، اللَّهُمَّ أَنْتَ الصَّاحِبُ فِي السَّفَرِ، وَالْخَليفَةُ فِي الْأَهْلِ، اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ وَعْثَاءِ السَّفَرِ، وَكَآبَةِ الْمَنْظَرِ، وَسُوءِ الْمُنْقَلَبِ فِي الْمَالِ وَالْأَهْلِ))، وإذا رَجَعَ قَالَهُنَّ وَزَادَ فِيهِنَّ: ((آيِبُونَ، تائِبُونَ، عَابِدُونَ، لِرَبِّنَا حَامِدُونَ)).
adhkar.translation: (Allah is the greatest, Allah is the greatest, Allah is the greatest, How perfect He is, The One Who has placed this (transport) at our service, and we ourselves would not have been capable of that, and to our Lord is our final destiny.  O Allah, we ask You for birr and taqwa in this journey of ours, and we ask You for deeds which please You.  O Allah, facilitate our journey and let us cover it’s distance quickly.  O Allah, You are The Companion on the journey and The Successor over the family, O Allah, I take refuge with You from the difficulties of travel, from having a change of hearts and being in a bad predicament, and I take refuge in You from an ill fated outcome with wealth and family.)(birr and taqwa: two comprehensive terms which individually, refer to all good actions and obedience i.e. performing the commanded actions and avoiding the prohibited actions.  When combined together, birr refers to doing those actions which have been commanded and taqwa refers to avoiding those actions which have been prohibited.)(A successor: one who succeeds another due to the latter’s absence or death.  This is the correct meaning of the word khaleefah; thus, it is incorrect to believe that Adam is the khaleefah (vicegerent, as is commonly translated) of Allah on earth because Allah is never absent, and will never die.  This supplication proves the correct understanding of this term and shows that Allah succeeds us and guards whom we leave behind when we die or are absent.)(upon returning the same supplication is recited with the following addition: (We return, repent, worship and praise our Lord.))
adhkar.transliteration: allahu akbar, allahu akbar, allahu akbar. subhanalladhi sakhkhara lana hadha wa ma kunna lahu muqrinin wa inna ila rabbina lamunqalibun. allahumma inna nas'aluka fi safarina hadhal-birra wat-taqwa wa minal-'amali ma tarda. allahumma hawwin 'alayna safarana hadha watwi 'anna bu'dah. allahumma antas-sahibu fis-safar. wal-khalifatu fil-ahl. allahumma inni a'udhu bika min wa'tha'is-safar wa ka'abatil-mandhari wa su'il-munqalabi fil-mali wal-ahl
adhkar.instruction: (Allahu akbar, Allahu akbar, Allahu akbar, subhanal-lathee sakhkhara lana hatha wama kunna lahu muqrineen, wa-inna ila rabbina lamunqaliboon, allahumma inna nas-aluka fee safarina hatha albirra wattaqwa, waminal-AAamali ma tarda, allahumma hawwin AAalayna safarana hatha, watwi AAanna buAAdah, allahumma antas-sahibu fis-safar, walkhaleefatu fil-ahl, allahumma innee aAAoothu bika min waAAtha-is-safar, waka-abatil-manthar, wasoo-il-munqalabi fil-mali wal-ahl.)(upon returning the same supplication is recited with the following addition: (Ayiboona, ta-iboona, AAabidoona, lirabbina hamidoon.))

adhkar.category_name: Invocation for entering a town or city
adhkar.arabic: ((اللَّهُمَّ رَبَّ السَّمَوَاتِ السَّبْعِ وَمَا أَظْلَلْنَ، وَرَبَّ الأَرَضِينَ السَّبْعِ وَمَا أَقْلَلْنَ، وَرَبَّ الشَّياطِينِ وَمَا أَضْلَلْنَ، وَرَبَّ الرِّيَاحِ وَمَا ذَرَيْنَ، أَسْأَلُكَ خَيْرَ هَذِهِ الْقَرْيَةِ، وَخَيْرَ أَهْلِهَا، وَخَيْرَ مَا فِيهَا، وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّهَا، وَشَرِّ أَهْلِهَا، وَشَرِّ مَا فِيهَا)).
adhkar.translation: (O Allah, Lord of the seven heavens and all that they envelop, Lord of the seven earths and all that they carry, Lord of the devils and all whom they misguide, Lord of the winds and all whom they whisk away. I ask You for the goodness of this village, the goodness of its inhabitants and for all the goodness found within it and I take refuge with You from the evil of this village, the evil of its inhabitants and from all the evil found within it.)
adhkar.transliteration: allahuma raba alsamawaati alsab'i wamaa aazlalna waraba alaaradiyna alsab'i wamaa aaqlalna waraba alshayatiyni wamaa aadlalna waraba alriyaahi wamaa dharayna aasaaluka khayra hadhihi alqaryahi wakhayra aahlihaa wakhayra maa fiyhaa waaa'uwdhu bika min sharihaa washari a
adhkar.instruction: (Allahumma rabbas-samawatis-sabAAi wama athlaln, warabbal-aradeenas-sabAAi wama aqlaln, warabbash-shayateeni wama adlaln, warabbar-riyahi wama tharayn, as-aluka khayra hathihil-qaryah, wakhayra ahlilha wakhayra ma feeha, wa-aAAoothu bika min sharriha washarri ahliha, washarri ma feeha.)

adhkar.arabic: ((لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لاَ شَرِيكَ لَهُ، لَهُ الْمُلْكُ، وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ، يُحْيِي وَيُمِيتُ، وَهُوَ حَيٌّ لاِ يَمُوتُ، بِيَدِهِ الْخَيْرُ، وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ)).
adhkar.translation: (None has the right to be worshipped except Allah, alone, without partner, to Him belongs all sovereignty and praise.  He gives life and causes death, and He is living and does not die.  In His hand is all good and He is over all things, omnipotent.)
adhkar.transliteration: la ilaha illa allah wahdahu la sharika lah, lahul-mulku wa lahul-hamdu wa huwa 'ala kulli shay'in qadir
adhkar.instruction: (La ilaha illal-lah,  wahdahu la shareeka lah, lahul-mulku walahul-hamd, yuhyee wayumeetu wahuwa hayyun la yamoot, biyadihil-khayru wahuwa  AAala kulli shayin qadeer.)

adhkar.category_name: Invocation for when your vehicle or mount begins to fail
adhkar.translation: (In the name of Allah.)
adhkar.transliteration: bismillah
adhkar.instruction: (Bismil-lah.)

adhkar.category_name: The traveler's invocation for the one he leaves behind
adhkar.arabic: ((أَسْتَوْدِعُكُمُ اللَّهَ الَّذِي لاَ تَضِيعُ وَدَائِعُهُ)).
adhkar.translation: (I place you in the trust of Allah, whose trust is never misplaced.)
adhkar.transliteration: aastawdi'ukumu allaha aladhiy laa tadiy'u wadaai'uhu.
adhkar.instruction: (AstawdiAAukumul-lah, allathee la tadeeAAu wada-iAAuh.)

adhkar.category_name: The resident's invocations for the traveler
adhkar.arabic: (( أَسْتَوْدِعُ اللَّهَ دِينَكَ، وَأَمَانَتَكَ، وَخَوَاتِيمَ عَمَلِكَ)).
adhkar.translation: (I place your religion, your faithfulness and the ends of your deeds in the trust of Allah.)
adhkar.transliteration: astawdi'ullaha dinaka, wa amanataka, wa khawatima 'amalik
adhkar.instruction: (AstawdiAAul-laha deenak, wa-amanatak, wakhawateema AAamalik.)

adhkar.category_name: The resident's invocations for the traveler
adhkar.arabic: ((زَوَّدَكَ اللَّهُ التَّقْوَى، وَغَفَرَ ذَنْبَكَ، وَيَسَّرَ لَكَ الخَيْرَ حَيْثُ ما كُنْتَ)).
adhkar.translation: (May Allah endow you with taqwa, forgive your sins and facilitate all good for you, wherever you be.)(taqwa: a comprehensive term which refers to all good actions and obedience i.e. performing the commanded actions and avoiding the prohibited actions.)
adhkar.transliteration: zawwadakallahut-taqwa, wa ghafara dhanbaka, wa yassara lakal-khayra haythuma kunta
adhkar.instruction: (Zawwadakal-lahut-taqwa, waghafara thanbak, wayassara lakal-khayra haythuma kunt.)

adhkar.category_name: Glorifying and magnifying Allah on the journey
adhkar.arabic: قَالَ جَابِرٌ رضي الله عنه: ((كُنَّا إِذَا صَعَدْنَا كَبَّرْنَا، وَإِذَا نَزَلْنَا سَبَّحْنَا)).
adhkar.translation: Jabir said: Whenever we went up a hill we would say Allaahu 'Akber (Allah is the Most Great) and when we descended we would say Subhaanallaah (Glory is to Allah)
adhkar.transliteration: qaala jaabirun rdy allh 'nh: kunaa iidhaa sa'adnaa kabarnaa waiidhaa nazalnaa sabahnaa.

adhkar.arabic: ((سَمَّعَ سَامِعٌ بِحَمْدِ اللَّهِ، وَحُسْنِ بَلاَئِهِ عَلَيْنَا، رَبَّنَا صاحِبْنَا، وَأَفْضِلْ عَلَيْنَا، عَائِذاً بِاللَّهِ مِنَ النَّارِ)).
adhkar.translation: (May a witness, be witness to our praise of Allah for His favours and bounties upon us.  Our Lord, protect us, show favour on us and deliver us from every evil.  I take refuge in Allah from the fire.)
adhkar.transliteration: sama'a saami'un bihamdi allahi wahusni balaaihi 'alaynaa rabanaa sahibnaa waaafdil 'alaynaa 'aaidhaan biallahi mina alnaari.
adhkar.instruction: (SamiAAa samiAAun bihamdil-lahi wahusni bala-ihi AAalayna. Rabbana sahibna wa-afdil AAalayna AAa-ithan billahi minan-nar.)

adhkar.category_name: Invocation for a layover (stopping along the way) on the journey
adhkar.arabic: ((أَعُوذُ بِكَلِمَاتِ اللَّهِ التَّامَّاتِ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا خَلَقَ)).
adhkar.translation: (I take refuge in Allah’s perfect words from the evil that He has created.)
adhkar.transliteration: a'udhu bikalimatillahit-tammati min sharri ma khalaq
adhkar.instruction: (aAAoothu bikalimatil-lahit-tammati min sharri ma khalaq.)

adhkar.category_name: What to say upon returning from a Journey
adhkar.arabic: ((يُكَبِّرُ عَلَى كُلِّ شَرَفٍ ثَلاَثَ تَكْبِيرَاتٍ ثُمَّ يَقُولُ: لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لاَ شَرِيكَ لَهُ، لَهُ الْمُلْكُ، وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ، وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ، آيِبُونَ، تَائِبُونَ، عَابِدُونَ، لِرَبِّنا حَامِدُونَ، صَدَقَ اللَّهُ وَعْدَهُ، وَنَصَرَ عَبْدَهُ، وَهَزَمَ الْأَحْزابَ وَحْدَهُ)).
adhkar.translation: (Ibn AAumar  reported that the Messenger of Allah  on return from a battle or from performing the pilgrimage would say at every high point: (Allah is the greatest, Allah is the greatest, Allah is the greatest.) and then he would say: (None has the right to be worshipped except Allah, alone, without partner.  To Him belongs all sovereignty and praise, and He is over all things omnipotent.  We return, repent, worship and praise our Lord.  Allah fulfilled His promise, aided His Servant, and single-handedly defeated the allies.))
adhkar.transliteration: yukabiru 'alaa kuli sharafin thalaatha takbiyraatin thuma yaquwlu: laa iilaha iilaa allahu wahdahu laa shariyka lahu lahu almulku walahu alhamdu wahuwa 'alaa kuli shay'in qadiyrun aayibuwna taaibuwna 'aabiduwna lirabina haamiduwna sadaqa allahu wa'dahu wanasara 'abdahu wahazama al
adhkar.instruction: (Ibn AAumar  reported that the Messenger of Allah  on return from a battle or from performing the pilgrimage would say at every high point: (Allahu akbar, Allahu akbar, Allahu akbar.) and then he would say: (La ilaha illal-lahu wahdahu la shareeka lah, lahul-mulku walahul-hamd, wahuwa AAala kulli shay-in qadeer, ayiboona ta-iboon, AAabidoon, lirabbina hamidoon, sadaqal-lahu waAAdah, wanasara AAabdah, wahazamal-ahzaba wahdah.))

adhkar.category_name: What to say if something happens to please you or to displease you
adhkar.arabic: كَانَ النَّبِيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم إِذَا أَتَاهُ الْأَمْرُ يَسُرُّهُ قَالَ: ((الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي بِنِعْمَتِهِ تَتِمُّ الصَّالِحَاتُ)) وَإِذَا أَتَاهُ الْأَمْرُ يَكْرَهُهُ قَالَ: ((الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ عَلَى كُلِّ حَالٍ)).
adhkar.translation: When something happened that pleased him, the Prophet used to say:((Alhamdu lillaahil-lathee bini'matihi tatimmuus-saalihaat)). Praise is to Allah Who by His blessings all good things are perfected. And if something happened that displeased him, he used to say:((Alhamdulillaahi 'alaa kulli haal)).Praise is to Allah in all circumstances.
adhkar.transliteration: kaana alnabiyu sla allh 'lyh wslm iidhaa aataahu alaamru yasuruhu qaala: alhamdu lilahi aladhiy bini'matihi tatimu alsaalihaatu waiidhaa aataahu alaamru yakrahuhu qaala: alhamdu lilahi 'alaa kuli haalin.
adhkar.instruction: When something happened that pleased him, the Prophet used to say:((Alhamdu lillaahil-lathee bini'matihi tatimmuus-saalihaat)).And if something happened that displeased him, he used to say:((Alhamdulillaahi 'alaa kulli haal)).

adhkar.category_name: The excellence of asking for Allah's blessings upon the Prophet pbuh
adhkar.arabic: قَالَ النَّبِيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((مَنْ صَلَّى عَلَيَّ صَلاَةً صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ بِهَا عَشْراً)).
adhkar.translation: (The Prophet (PBUH) said: ‘Whoever sends a prayer upon me, Allah sends ten upon him.)
adhkar.transliteration: qaala alnabiyu sla allh 'lyh wslm: man salaa 'alaya salaahan salaa allahu 'alayhi bihaa 'ashraan.

adhkar.category_name: The excellence of asking for Allah's blessings upon the Prophet pbuh
adhkar.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((لاَ تَجْعَلُوا قَبْرِي عِيداً وَصَلُّوا عَلَيَّ؛ فَإِنَّ صَلاَتَكُم تَبْلُغُنِي حَيْثُ كُنْتُمْ)).
adhkar.translation: (He (PBUH) also said: ‘Do not take my grave as a place of habitual ceremony.  Send prayers upon me, for verily your prayers reach me wherever you are.)
adhkar.transliteration: waqaala sla allh 'lyh wslm: laa taj'aluwa qabriy 'iydaan wasaluwa 'alaya faiina salaatakum tablughuniy haythu kuntum.

adhkar.category_name: The excellence of asking for Allah's blessings upon the Prophet pbuh
adhkar.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((الْبَخِيلُ مَنْ ذُكِرْتُ عِنْدَهُ فَلَمْ يُصَلِّ عَلَيَّ)).
adhkar.translation: (He (PBUH) also said: ‘A miser is one whom when I am mentioned to him,  fails to send prayers upon me.)
adhkar.transliteration: waqaala sla allh 'lyh wslm: albakhiylu man dhukirtu 'indahu falam yusali 'alaya.

adhkar.category_name: The excellence of asking for Allah's blessings upon the Prophet pbuh
adhkar.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم:((إِنَّ لِلَّهِ مَلاَئِكَةً سَيَّاحِينَ فِي الْأَرْضِ يُبَلِّغُونِي مِنْ أُمَّتِي السَّلاَمَ)).
adhkar.translation: The Prophet [PBUH] said:'Indeed Allah has angels who roam the earth and they convey to me the greetings (or prayers of peace) of my 'Ummah (nation).
adhkar.transliteration: waqaala sla allh 'lyh wslm:iina lilahi malaaikahan sayaahiyna fiy alaardi yubalighuwniy min aumatiy alsalaama.

adhkar.category_name: The excellence of asking for Allah's blessings upon the Prophet pbuh
adhkar.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((مَا مِنْ أَحَدٍ يُسَلِّمُ عَلَيَّ إِلاَّ رَدَّ اللَّهُ عَلَيَّ رُوحِيَ حَتَّى أَرُدَّ عَلَيْهِ السَّلاَمَ)).
adhkar.translation: The Prophet [PBUH] said: 'No one sends greetings (or prayers of peace) upon me but Allah returns my soul to me so that I may return his greetings
adhkar.transliteration: waqaala sla allh 'lyh wslm: maa min aahadin yusalimu 'alaya iilaa rada allahu 'alaya ruwhiya hataa aaruda 'alayhi alsalaama.

adhkar.arabic: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((لاَ تَدْخُلُوا الْجَنَّةَ حَتَّى تُؤْمِنُوا، وَلاَ تُؤْمِنُوا حَتَّى تَحَابُّوا، أَوَلاَ أَدُلُّكُم عَلَى شَيْءٍ إِذَا فَعَلْتُمُوهُ تَحَابَبْتُم، أَفْشُوا السَّلاَمَ بَيْنَكُمْ)).
adhkar.translation: (The Messenger of Allah  said: ‘You shall not enter paradise until you believe, and you shall not believe until you love one another. Shall I not inform you of something, if you were to act upon it, you will indeed achieve mutual love for one another?  Spread the greeting amongst yourselves.)
adhkar.transliteration: qaala rasuwlu allahi sla allh 'lyh wslm: laa tadkhuluwa aljanaha hataa tuminuwa walaa tuminuwa hataa tahaabuwa aawalaa aadulukum 'alaa shay'in iidhaa fa'altumuwhu tahaababtum aafshuwa alsalaama baynakum.

adhkar.arabic: ((ثَلاَثٌ مَنْ جَمَعَهُنَّ فَقَدْ جَمَعَ الْإِيمَانَ: الْإِنْصَافُ مِنْ نَفْسِكَ، وَبَذْلُ السَّلاَمِ لِلْعَالَمِ، وَالْإِنْفَاقُ مِنَ الإِقْتَارِ)).
adhkar.translation: (Aammar  said: ‘Three characteristics, whoever combines them, has completed his faith: to be just, to spread greetings to all people and to spend (charitably) out of the little you have.)
adhkar.transliteration: thalaathun man jama'ahuna faqad jama'a aliiymaana: aliinsaafu min nafsika wabadhlu alsalaami lil'aalami waaliinfaaqu mina aliiqtaari.

adhkar.arabic: وَعَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُمَرَ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمَا: أنَّ رَجُلاً سَأَلَ النَّبِيَّ صلى الله عليه وسلم أيُّ الْإِسْلاَمِ خَيْرٌ قَالَ: ((تُطْعِمُ الطَّعَامَ، وَتَقْرأُ السَّلاَمَ عَلَى مَنْ عَرَفْتَ وَمَنْ لَمْ تَعْرِفْ)).
adhkar.translation: (Aabdullah Ibn Aamr  reported that a man asked the Prophet : ‘Which Islam is the best?’.  He  replied: Feed (the poor), and greet those whom you know as well as those whom you do not.)
adhkar.transliteration: wa'an 'abdi allahi bni 'umara radiya allahu 'anhumaa: ana rajulaan saaala alnabiya sla allh 'lyh wslm ayu aliislaami khayrun qaala: tut'imu alta'aama wataqrau alsalaama 'alaa man 'arafta waman lam ta'rif.

adhkar.category_name: How to reply to a disbeliever if he says Salam to you
adhkar.arabic: ((إذَا سَلَّمَ عَلَيْكُمْ أَهْلُ الْكِتَابِ فَقُولُوا: وَعَلَيْكُمْ)).
adhkar.translation: (When the people of the Book greet you, reply by saying: (And upon you.))
adhkar.transliteration: idhaa salama 'alaykum aahlu alkitaabi faquwluwa: wa'alaykum.

adhkar.category_name: Invocation upon hearing the cock's crow or the bray of a donkey
adhkar.arabic: ((إِذَا سَمِعْتُمْ صِيَاحَ الدِّيَكَةِ فَاسْأَلُوا اللَّهَ مِنْ فَضْلِهِ؛ فَإِنَّهَا رَأَتْ مَلَكاً وَإِذَا سَمِعْتُمْ نَهِيقَ الْحِمَارِ فَتَعَوَّذُوا بِاللَّهِ مِنَ الشَّيطَانِ؛ فَإِنَّهُ رَأَى شَيْطَاناً)).
adhkar.translation: (If you hear the crow of a rooster, ask Allah for his bounty for it has seen an angel and if you hear the braying of an ass, seek refuge in Allah for it has seen a devil.)
adhkar.transliteration: iidhaa sami'tum siyaaha aldiyakahi faasaaluwa allaha min fadlihi faiinahaa raaat malakaan waiidhaa sami'tum nahiyqa alhimaari fata'awadhuwa biallahi mina alshaytaani faiinahu raaaa shaytaanaan.

adhkar.category_name: Invocation upon hearing a dog barking in the night
adhkar.arabic: ((إِذَا سَمِعْتُمْ نُبَاحَ الْكِلاَبِ وَنَهِيقَ الْحَمِيرِ بِاللَّيْلِ فَتَعَوَّذُوا بِاللَّهِ مِنْهُنَّ؛ فَإِنَّهُنَّ يَرَيْنَ مَا لاَ تَرَوْنَ)).
adhkar.translation: (If you hear the barking of dogs or the braying of asses at night, seek refuge in Allah for they see what you do not.)
adhkar.transliteration: iidhaa sami'tum nubaaha alkilaabi wanahiyqa alhamiyri biallayli fata'awadhuwa biallahi minhuna faiinahuna yarayna maa laa tarawna.

adhkar.category_name: Invocation for someone you have spoken ill to
adhkar.arabic: قَالَ النَّبِيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((اللَّهُمَّ فَأَيُّمَا مُؤْمِنٍ سَبَبْتُهُ فَاجْعَلْ ذَلِكَ لَهُ قُرْبَةً إِلَيْكَ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ)).
adhkar.translation: (O Allah, to any believer whom I have insulted, let that be cause to draw him near to You on the Day of Resurrection.)
adhkar.transliteration: qaala alnabiyu sla allh 'lyh wslm: allahuma faaayumaa muminin sababtuhu faaj'al dhalika lahu qurbahan iilayka yawma alqiyaamahi.
adhkar.instruction: (Allahumma fa-ayyuma mu/minin sababtuhu fajAAal thalika lahu qurbatan ilayka yawmal-qiyamah.)

adhkar.category_name: How a Muslim should praise another Muslim
adhkar.arabic: قَالَ النَّبِيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((إِذَا كَانَ أَحَدُكُم مَادِحاً صَاحِبَهُ لاَ مَحَالَةَ فَلْيَقُلْ: أَحْسِبُ فُلاَناً وَاللَّهُ حَسِيبُهُ، وَلاَ أُزَكِّي عَلَى اللَّهِ أَحَداً، أَحْسِبُهُ – إِنْ كَانَ يَعْلَمُ ذَاكَ – كَذَا وَكَذَا)).
adhkar.translation: (He  said: ‘If anyone of you is impelled to praise his brother, then he should say: ‘I deem so-and-so to be…and Allah is his reckoner…and I don’t praise anyone, putting it (i.e. my praising) forward, in front of Allah’s commendation, however I assume him so and so’…if he knows that of him.)
adhkar.transliteration: qaala alnabiyu sla allh 'lyh wslm: iidhaa kaana aahadukum maadihaan saahibahu laa mahaalaha falyaqul: aahsibu fulaanaan waallahu hasiybuhu walaa auzakiy 'alaa allahi aahadaan aahsibuhu iin kaana ya'lamu dhaaka kadhaa wakadhaa.

adhkar.category_name: What a Muslim should say when he is praised
adhkar.arabic: ((اللَّهُمَّ لاَ تُؤَاخِذْنِي بِمَا يَقُولُونَ، وَاغْفِرْ لِي مَا لاَ يَعْلَمُونَ، [وَاجْعَلْنِي خَيْراً مِمَّا يَظُّنُّونَ])).
adhkar.translation: O Allah, do not call me to account for what they say and forgive me for what they have no knowledge of [and make me better than they imagine].
adhkar.transliteration: allahuma laa tuaakhidhniy bimaa yaquwluwna waaghfir liy maa laa ya'lamuwna waaj'alniy khayraan mimaa yazunuwna.
adhkar.instruction: Allaahumma laa tu'aakhithnee bimaa yaqooloona, waghfir lee maa laa ya'lamoona [waj'alnee khayram-mimmaa yadhunnoon]

adhkar.category_name: The pilgrim's announcement of his arrival for Hajj or Umrah
adhkar.arabic: ((لَبَّيْكَ اللَّهُمَّ لَبَّيْكَ، لَبَّيْكَ لاَ شَرِيكَ لَكَ لَبَّيْكَ، إِنَّ الْحَمْدَ، وَالنِّعْمَةَ، لَكَ وَالْمُلْكَ، لاَ شَرِيكَ لَكَ)).
adhkar.translation: (Here I am O Allah, (in response to Your call), here I am. Here I am, You have no partner, here I am.  Verily all praise, grace and sovereignty belong to You. You have no partner.)
adhkar.transliteration: labayka allahuma labayka labayka laa shariyka laka labayka iina alhamda waalni'maha laka waalmulka laa shariyka laka.
adhkar.instruction: (Labbaykal-lahumma labbayk, labbayka la shareeka laka labbayk, innal-hamda wanniAAmata laka walmulk, la shareeka lak.)

adhkar.category_name: Saying Allahu Akbar when passing the Black Stone
adhkar.arabic: ((طَافَ النَّبيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم بِالْبَيْتِ عَلَى بَعِيرٍ كُلَّمَا أَتَى الرُّكْنَ أَشَارَ إِلَيْهِ بِشَيْءٍ عِنْدَهُ وَكَبَّرَ)).
adhkar.translation: (The Prophet  circled the KaAAbah on a camel, every time he reached the black stone he would point to it with his staff and say: (Allah is the greatest))
adhkar.transliteration: taafa alnabyu sla allh 'lyh wslm bialbayti 'alaa ba'iyrin kulamaa aataa alrukna aashaara iilayhi bishay'in 'indahu wakabara.
adhkar.instruction: (The Prophet  circled the KaAAbah on a camel, every time he reached the black stone he would point to it with his staff and say: (Allahu akbar))

adhkar.category_name: Invocation to be recited between the Yemenite Corner and the Black Stone
adhkar.arabic: ((﴿رَبَّنَا آتِنَا فِي الدُّنْيَا حَسَنَةً وَفِي الْآخِرَةِ حَسَنَةً وَقِنَا عَذَابَ النَّارِ﴾)).
adhkar.translation: The Prophet PBUH used to say between the Yemeni corner and the black stone: (O our Lord, grant us the best in this life and the best in the next life, and protect us from the punishment of the Fire.)
adhkar.transliteration: rabbana atina fid-dunya hasanatan wa fil-akhirati hasanatan wa qina 'adhaban-nar
adhkar.instruction: The Prophet PBUH used to say between the Yemeni corner and the black stone: (Rabbana atina fee alddunya hasanatan wafee al-akhirati hasanatan waqina  AAathaba alnnar)[Al-Baqarah: 201]

adhkar.category_name: Invocation to be recited while standing at Safa and Marwah
adhkar.arabic: لَمَّا دَنَا النَّبِيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم مِنَ الصَّفَا قَرَأَ: ﴿إِنَّ الصَّفَا وَالْمَرْوَةَ مِنْ شَعَآئِرِ اللَّهِ﴾ أَبْدَأُ بِمَا بَدَأَ اللَّهُ بِهِ)) فَبَدَأَ بِالصَّفَا فَرَقِيَ عَلَيْهِ حَتَّى رَأَى الْبَيْتَ، فَاسْتَقْبَلَ الْقِبْلَةَ، فَوَحَّدَ اللَّهَ وَكبَّرَهُ وَقَالَ: ((لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لاَ شَرِيكَ لَهُ، لَهُ الْمُلْكُ وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ، لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ، أَنْجَزَ وَعْدَهُ، وَنَصَرَ عَبْدَهُ، وَهَزَمَ الْأَحْزَابَ وَحْدَهُ، ثُمَّ دَعَا بَيْنَ ذلكَ. قَالَ مِثْلَ هَذَا ثَلاَثَ مَرَّاتٍ)) الْحَدِيثُ. وَفِيهِ: ((فَفَعَلَ عَلَى الْمَرْوَةِ كَمَا فَعَلَ عَلَى الصَّفَا)).
adhkar.translation: (Jabir  said when describing the Prophet’s  pilgrimage: ‘…and when he approached mount Safa he recited: (Indeed Safa and Marwah are from the places of worship of Allah…) (I begin with what Allah began with.) so he started with Safa and climed it until he could see the KaAAbah, he then faced it and said: (Allah is the greatest, Allah is the greatest, Allah is the greatest.) and then he would say the following three times making a supplication (one should make a personal supplication) after each time: (None has the right to be worshipped except Allah, alone, without partner.To Him belongs all sovereignty and praise and He is over all things amnipotent. None has the right to be worshipped except Allah alone.  He fulfilled His promise, aided His Servant and single-handedly defeated the allies.)) (he  would repeat this action at Marwah.)
adhkar.transliteration: lamaa danaa alnabiyu sla allh 'lyh wslm mina alsafaa qaraaa: iina alsafaa waalmarwaha min sha'aaairi allahi aabdaau bimaa badaaa allahu bihi fabadaaa bialsafaa faraqiya 'alayhi hataa raaaa albayta faastaqbala alqiblaha fawahada allaha wakbarahu waqaala: laa iilaha iila
adhkar.instruction: (Jabir  said when describing the Prophet’s  pilgrimage: ‘…and when he approached mount Safa he recited: ( Innas-safa wa-almarwata min shaAAa-iri Allah ….) (Abda-o bima badaal-lahu bih.) so he started with Safa and climed it until he could see the KaAAbah, he then faced it and said: (Allahu akbar, Allahu akbar, Allahu akbar.) and then he would say the following three times making a supplication (one should make a personal supplication) after each time: (La ilaha illal-lahu wahdahu la shareeka lah, lahul-mulku walahul-hamd, wahuwa AAala kulli shayin qadeer, la ilaha illal-lahu wahdah, anjaza waAAdah, wanasara AAabdah, wahazamal -ahzaba wahdah.)(he  would repeat this action at Marwah.)

adhkar.category_name: Invocation to be recited on the Day of Arafat
adhkar.arabic: قَالَ النَّبِيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((خَيْرُ الدُّعَاءِ دُعَاءُ يَوْمِ عَرَفَةَ، وَخَيْرُ مَا قُلْتُ أَنَا وَالنَّبيُّونَ مِنْ قَبْلِي: لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لاَ شَرِيكَ لَهُ، لَهُ الْمُلْكُ وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ)).
adhkar.translation: (The best of supplications is the supplication on the day of AAarafah and the best which I and the Prophets before me have said (is): (None has the right to be worshipped except Allah, alone, without partner. To Him belongs all praise and sovereignty and He is over all things omnipotent.))
adhkar.transliteration: qaala alnabiyu sla allh 'lyh wslm: khayru aldu'aa'i du'aa'u yawmi 'arafaha wakhayru maa qultu aanaa waalnabyuwna min qabliy: laa iilaha iilaa allahu wahdahu laa shariyka lahu lahu almulku walahu alhamdu wahuwa 'alaa kuli shay'in qadiyrun.
adhkar.instruction: (The best of supplications is the supplication on the day of AAarafah and the best which I and the Prophets before me have said (is): (La ilaha illal-lahu wahdahu la shareeka lah, lahul-mulku walahul-hamd, wahuwa AAala kulli shayin qadeer.))

adhkar.category_name: Supplication to be recited at the sacred area of Muzdalifah
adhkar.arabic: رَكِبَ النَّبِيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم الْقَصْوَاءَ حَتَّى أَتَى الْمَشْعَرَ الْحَرَامَ فَاسْتَقْبَلَ الْقِبْلَةَ (فَدَعَاهُ، وَكَبَّرَهُ، وَهَللَّهُ، وَوَحَّدَهُ) فَلَمْ يَزَلْ وَاقِفاً حَتَّى أَسْفَرَ جِدَّاً فَدَفَعَ قَبْلَ أَنْ تَطْلُعَ الشَّمسُ.
adhkar.translation: The Prophet rode his camel, Al-Qaswa, until he reached the sacred area (Al-Mash aril Haraam). Then he faced the Qiblah and invoked Allah, and repeatedly said the words: Allaahu Akbar (Allah is the Most Great), Allaahu Ahad (Allah is One) and Laa ilaaha illallaah (There is none worthy of worship but Allah). He remained stationary until the sky became yellow with the dawn, and then pressed on before sunrise.
adhkar.transliteration: rakiba alnabiyu sla allh 'lyh wslm alqaswaa'a hataa aataa almash'ara alharaama faastaqbala alqiblaha fada'aahu wakabarahu wahallahu wawahadahu falam yazal waaqifaan hataa aasfara jidaaan fadafa'a qabla aan tatlu'a alshamsu.

adhkar.category_name: Saying Allahu Akbar while stoning the three pillars at Mina
adhkar.arabic: ((يُكَبِّرُ كُلَّمَا رَمَى بِحَصَاةٍ عِنْدَ الْجِمَارِ الثَّلاَثِ، ثُمَّ يَتَقَدَّمُ، ويَقِفُ يَدْعُو مُسْتَقْبِلَ الْقِبلَةِ، رَافِعاً يَدَيْهِ بَعْدَ الْجَمْرَةِ الْأُولَى وَالثَّانِيَةِ. أَمَّا جَمْرَةُ الْعَقَبَةِ فَيَرْمِيهَا وَيُكَبِّرُ عِنْدَ كُلِّ حَصَاةٍ وَيَنْصَرِفُ وَلاَ يَقِفُ عِنْدَهَا)).
adhkar.translation: The Prophet PBUH said Allaahu 'Akbar (Allah is the Most Great) with each pebble he threw at the three pillars. Then he went forward, stood facing the Qiblah and raised his hands and called upon Allah. That was after (stoning) the first and second pillar As for the third, he stoned it and called out Allahu Akbar with every pebble he threw but when he was finished he left without standing at it (for supplications).
adhkar.transliteration: yukabiru kulamaa ramaa bihasaahin 'inda aljimaari althalaathi thuma yataqadamu wyaqifu yad'uw mustaqbila alqiblahi raafi'aan yadayhi ba'da aljamrahi alauwlaa waalthaaniyahi. aamaa jamrahu al'aqabahi fayarmiyhaa wayukabiru 'inda kuli hasaahin wayansarifu walaa yaqifu 'indahaa

adhkar.category_name: What to say when surprised or startled
adhkar.arabic: ((سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ!)).
adhkar.translation: (How perfect Allah is.)
adhkar.transliteration: subhanallah
adhkar.instruction: (Subhanal-lah!.)

adhkar.category_name: What to say when surprised or startled
adhkar.translation: (Allah is the greatest.)
adhkar.transliteration: allahu akbar
adhkar.instruction: (Allahu akbar.)

adhkar.category_name: What to say when something that pleases you happens
adhkar.arabic: ((كَانَ النَّبيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم إِذَا أَتَاهُ أَمْرٌ يَسُرُّهُ أَوْ يُسَرُّ بِهِ خَرَّ سَاجِداً شُكْراً لِلَّهِ تَبَارَكَ وَتَعَالَى)).
adhkar.translation: (The Prophet PBUH would prostrate in gratitude to Allah upon receiving news which pleased him or which caused pleasure. )
adhkar.transliteration: kaana alnabyu sla allh 'lyh wslm iidhaa aataahu aamrun yasuruhu aaw yusaru bihi khara saajidaan shukraan lilahi tabaaraka wata'aalaa.

adhkar.category_name: What to say when you feel a pain in your body
adhkar.arabic: ((ضَعْ يَدَكَ عَلَى الَّذِي تَألَّمَ مِنْ جَسَدِكَ وَقُلْ: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ، ثَلاَثاً، وَقُلْ سَبْعَ مَرَّاتٍ: أَعُوذُ بِاللَّهِ وَقُدْرَتِهِ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا أَجِدُ وَأُحَاذِرُ)).
adhkar.translation: (Place your hand at the site of the pain and say: (In the name of Allah(three times)) the supplicate seven times: (I take refuge in Allah and within His omnipotence from the evil that I feel and am wary of.))
adhkar.transliteration: da' yadaka 'alaa aladhiy taalama min jasadika waqul: bismi allahi thalaathaan waqul sab'a maraatin: aa'uwdhu biallahi waqudratihi min shari maa aajidu waauhaadhiru.
adhkar.instruction: (Place your hand at the site of the pain and say: (Bismil-lah (three times)) the supplicate seven times: (aAAoothu billahi waqudratih min sharri ma ajidu wa-ohathir.))

adhkar.category_name: What to say when you fear you may afflict something with the evil eye
adhkar.arabic: ((إِذَا رَأَى أَحَدُكُم مِنْ أَخِيهِ، أَوْ مِنْ نَفْسِهِ، أَوْ مِنْ مَالِهِ مَا يُعْجِبُهُ [فَلْيَدْعُ لَهُ بِالْبَرَكَةِ] فَإِنَّ الْعَيْنَ حَقٌّ)).
adhkar.translation: If you see anything of your brother that pleases you, or of his person or of his property [then ask Allah to bless him in it] for the envious eye is real.
adhkar.transliteration: iidhaa raaaa aahadukum min aakhiyhi aaw min nafsihi aaw min maalihi maa yu'jibuhu falyad'u lahu bialbarakahi faiina al'ayna haqun.

adhkar.category_name: What to say when you feel frightened
adhkar.arabic: ((لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ!)).
adhkar.translation: (None has the right to be worshipped except Allah.)
adhkar.transliteration: laa iilaha iilaa allahu!.
adhkar.instruction: (La ilaha illal-lah.)

adhkar.category_name: What to say when slaughtering or sacrificing an animal
adhkar.arabic: ((بِسْمِ اللَّهِ وَاللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ [اللَّهُمَّ مِنْكَ وَلَكَ] اللَّهُمَّ تَقَبَّلْ مِنِّي)).
adhkar.translation: (In the name of Allah, and Allah is the greatest.  O Allah, (it is) from You and belongs to You, O Allah, accept this from me.)
adhkar.transliteration: bismi allahi waallahu aakbaru allahuma minka walaka allahuma taqabal miniy.
adhkar.instruction: (Bismil-lah wallahu akbar, allahumma minka walak, allahumma taqabbal  minnee.)

adhkar.category_name: What to say to foil the devil's plots
adhkar.arabic: ((أَعُوذُ بكَلِمَاتِ اللَّهِ التَّامَّاتِ الَّتِي لاَ يُجَاوِزُهُنَّ بَرٌّ وَلاَ فَاجِرٌ: مِنْ شَرِّ مَا خَلَقَ، وَبَرَأَ وَذَرَأَ، وَمِنْ شَرِّ مَا يَنْزِلُ مِنَ السَّمَاءِ، وَمِنْ شَرِّ مَا يَعْرُجُ فيهَا، وَمِنْ شَرِّ مَا ذَرَأَ فِي الْأَرْضِ، وَمِنْ شَرِّ مَا يَخْرُجُ مِنْهَا، وَمِنْ شَرِّ فِتَنِ اللَّيْلِ وَالنَّهَارِ، وَمِنْ شَرِّ كُلِّ طَارِقٍ إِلاَّ طَارِقاً يَطْرُقُ بِخَيْرٍ يَا رَحْمَنُ)).
adhkar.translation: (I take refuge within Allah’s perfect words which no righteous or unrighteous person can transgress, from all the evil that He has created, made and originated.  (I take refuge) from the evil that descends from the sky and the evil that rises up to it.  (I take refuge) from the evil that is spread on Earth and the evil that springs from her, and I take refuge from the evil of the tribulations of night and day, and the evil of one who visits at night except the one who brings good, O Merciful One.)
adhkar.transliteration: aa'uwdhu bkalimaati allahi altaamaati alatiy laa yujaawizuhuna barun walaa faajirun: min shari maa khalaqa wabaraaa wadharaaa wamin shari maa yanzilu mina alsamaa'i wamin shari maa ya'ruju fyhaa wamin shari maa dharaaa fiy alaardi wamin shari maa yakhruju minhaa wamin shari fitan
adhkar.instruction: (aAAoothu bikalimatil-lahit-tammat, allatee la yujawizuhunna barrun wala fajir min sharri ma khalaq, wabaraa watharaa, wamin sharri ma yanzilu minas-sama/, wamin sharri ma yaAAruju feeha, wamin sharri ma tharaa fil-ard, wamin sharri ma yakhruju minha, wamin sharri fitnanil-layli wannahar, wamin sharri kulli tariqin illa tariqan yatruqu bikhayrin ya Rahman.)

adhkar.arabic: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((وَاللَّهِ إِنِّي لأَسْتَغفِرُ اللَّهَ وَأَتُوبُ إِلَيْهِ فِي الْيَوْمِ أَكْثَرَ مِنْ سَبْعِينَ مَرَّةٍ)).
adhkar.translation: (The Messenger of Allah  said: ‘By Allah, I seek forgiveness and repent to Allah, more than seventy times a day.)
adhkar.transliteration: qaala rasuwlu allahi sla allh 'lyh wslm: waallahi iiniy laastaghfiru allaha waaatuwbu iilayhi fiy alyawmi aakthara min sab'iyna marahin.

adhkar.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ تُوبُوا إِلَى اللَّهِ فَإِنِّي أَتُوبُ فِي الْيَوْمِ إِلَيْهِ مِائَةَ مَرَّةٍ)).
adhkar.translation: (He  also said: ‘O People, Repent! Verily I repent to Allah, a hundred times a day.)
adhkar.transliteration: waqaala sla allh 'lyh wslm: yaa aayuhaa alnaasu tuwbuwa iilaa allahi faiiniy aatuwbu fiy alyawmi iilayhi miaaha marahin.

adhkar.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((مَنْ قَالَ أَسْتَغْفِرُ اللَّهَ الْعَظيمَ الَّذِي لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ هُوَ الْحَيُّ القَيّوُمُ وَأَتُوبُ إِلَيهِ، غَفَرَ اللَّهُ لَهُ وَإِنْ كَانَ فَرَّ مِنَ الزَّحْفِ)).
adhkar.translation: (He  also said: ‘Whoever says:(I seek Allah’s forgiveness, besides whom, none has the right to be worshipped except He, The Ever Living, The Self-Subsisting and Supporter of all, and I turn to Him in repentance.) Allah would forgive him even if he was one who fled during the advance of an army.)
adhkar.transliteration: waqaala sla allh 'lyh wslm: man qaala aastaghfiru allaha al'azyma aladhiy laa iilaha iilaa huwa alhayu alqaywumu waaatuwbu iilayhi ghafara allahu lahu waiin kaana fara mina alzahfi.
adhkar.instruction: (He  also said: ‘Whoever says:(Astaghfirul-lahal-lathee la ilaha illa huwal-hayyul-qayyoomu wa-atoobu ilayh.) Allah would forgive him even if he was one who fled during the advance of an army.)

adhkar.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((أَقْرَبُ مَا يَكُونُ الرَّبُّ مِنَ الْعَبْدِ فِي جَوْفِ اللَّيْلِ الآخِرِ فَإِنِ اسْتَطَعْتَ أَنْ تَكُونَ مِمَّنْ يَذْكُرُ اللَّهَ فِي تِلْكَ السَّاعَةِ فَكُنْ)).
adhkar.translation: (He  said: ‘The nearest the Lord comes to His servant is in the middle of the night, so if you are able to be of those who remember Allah at that time, then be so.)
adhkar.transliteration: waqaala sla allh 'lyh wslm: aaqrabu maa yakuwnu alrabu mina al'abdi fiy jawfi allayli alaakhiri faiini astata'ta aan takuwna miman yadhkuru allaha fiy tilka alsaa'ahi fakun.

adhkar.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((أَقْرَبُ مَا يَكُونُ الْعَبْدُ مِنْ رَبِّهِ وَهُوَ سَاجِدٌ فَأَكثِرُوا الدُّعَاءَ)).
adhkar.translation: (He  also said: ‘The nearest a servant is to his Lord is when he is prostrating, so supplicate much therein.)
adhkar.transliteration: waqaala sla allh 'lyh wslm: aaqrabu maa yakuwnu al'abdu min rabihi wahuwa saajidun faaakthiruwa aldu'aa'a.

adhkar.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((إِنَّهُ لَيُغَانُ عَلَى قَلْبِي وَإِنِّي لأَسْتَغْفِرُ اللَّهَ فِي الْيَوْمِ مِائَةَ مَرَّةٍ)).
adhkar.translation: (He  also said: ‘verily my heart becomes preoccupied, and verily I seek Allah’s forgiveness a hundred times a day.)(preoccupied: i.e. in a state of ‘forgetfulness’. The Prophet  always used to increase in his remembrance of his Lord, in attaining a nearness to Allah and having consciousness of Allah to the extent that if this intensity lessened in anyway, he would regard it as a sin and would then race to seek forgiveness from Allah.)
adhkar.transliteration: waqaala sla allh 'lyh wslm: iinahu layughaanu 'alaa qalbiy waiiniy laastaghfiru allaha fiy alyawmi miaaha marahin.

adhkar.arabic: قَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم مَنْ قَالَ: سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ وَبِحَمْدِهِ فِي يَوْمٍ مِائَةَ مَرَّةٍ حُطَّتْ خَطَايَاهُ وَلَوْ كَانَتْ مِثْلَ زَبَدِ الْبَحْر)).
adhkar.translation: (Whoever says:(How perfect Allah is and I praise Him.) a hundred times during the day, his sins are wiped away, even if they are like the foam of the sea.)
adhkar.transliteration: qaala sla allh 'lyh wslm man qaala: subhaana allahi wabihamdihi fiy yawmin miaaha marahin hutat khataayaahu walaw kaanat mithla zabadi albahr.
adhkar.instruction: (Whoever says:(Subhanal-lahi wabihamdih.) a hundred times during the day, his sins are wiped away, even if they are like the foam of the sea.)

adhkar.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((مَنْ قَالَ لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لاَ شَرِيكَ لَهُ، لَهُ الْمُلْكُ، وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ، وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ عَشْرَ مِرَارٍ، كَانَ كَمَنْ أَعْتَقَ أَرْبَعَةَ أَنْفُسٍ مِنْ وَلَدِ إِسْمَاعِيلَ)).
adhkar.translation: (Abu Hurayrah  reported that the Messenger of Allah  said: ‘Whoever says: (None has the right to be worshipped except Allah, alone, without partner. To Him belongs all sovereignty and praise and He is over all things omnipotent.) a hundred times during the day, has the reward of freeing ten slaves, a hundred good deeds are recorded for him and a hundred bad deeds are wiped away and he has gained refuge from the devil that day until evening and none shall come with anything better except someone who has done more.)
adhkar.transliteration: waqaala sla allh 'lyh wslm: man qaala laa iilaha iilaa allahu wahdahu laa shariyka lahu lahu almulku walahu alhamdu wahuwa 'alaa kuli shay'in qadiyrun 'ashra miraarin kaana kaman aa'taqa aarba'aha aanfusin min waladi iismaa'iyla.
adhkar.instruction: (Abu Hurayrah  reported that the Messenger of Allah  said: ‘Whoever says: (La ilaha illal-lahu wahdahu la shareeka lah, lahul-mulku walahul-hamd, wahuwa AAala kulli shayin qadeer.) a hundred times during the day, has the reward of freeing ten slaves, a hundred good deeds are recorded for him and a hundred bad deeds are wiped away and he has gained refuge from the devil that day until evening and none shall come with anything better except someone who has done more.)

adhkar.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((كَلِمَتَانِ خَفِيفَتَانِ عَلَى اللِّسَانِ، ثَقِيلَتَانِ فِي الْمِيزَانِ، حَبِيبَتَانِ إِلَى الرَّحْمَنِ: سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ وَبِحَمْدِهِ، سُبْحانَ اللَّهِ الْعَظِيمِ)).
adhkar.translation: (Aboo Hurayrah  reported that the Messenger of Allah  said:’(There are) Two words, (which are ) light on the tongue, heavy on the Scale and beloved to The Most Gracious: (How perfect Allah is and I praise Him. How perfect Allah is, The Supreme.))
adhkar.transliteration: waqaala sla allh 'lyh wslm: kalimataani khafiyfataani 'alaa allisaani thaqiylataani fiy almiyzaani habiybataani iilaa alrahmani: subhaana allahi wabihamdihi subhana allahi al'aziymi.
adhkar.instruction: (Aboo Hurayrah  reported that the Messenger of Allah  said:’(There are) Two words, (which are ) light on the tongue, heavy on the Scale and beloved to The Most Gracious: (Subhanal-lahi wabihamdih, wasubhanal-lahil-AAatheem.))

adhkar.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((لَأَنْ أَقُولَ سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ، وَالْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ، وَلاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ، وَاللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، أَحَبُّ إِلَيَّ مِمَّا طَلَعَتْ عَلَيْهِ الشَّمسُ)).
adhkar.translation: (Aboo Hurayrah  reported that the Messenger of Allah  said: ‘Saying: (How perfect Allah is, and all praise is for Allah. None has the right to be worshipped except Allah, and Allah is the greatest.) is more beloved to me than everything the sun has risen over.)
adhkar.transliteration: waqaala sla allh 'lyh wslm: laaan aaquwla subhaana allahi waalhamdu lilahi walaa iilaha iilaa allahu waallahu aakbaru aahabu iilaya mimaa tala'at 'alayhi alshamsu.
adhkar.instruction: (Aboo Hurayrah  reported that the Messenger of Allah  said: ‘Saying: (Subhanal-lah, walhamdu lillah, la ilaha illal-lah wallahu akbar.) is more beloved to me than everything the sun has risen over.)

adhkar.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((أَيَعْجِزُ أَحَدُكُم أَنْ يَكْسِبَ كُلَّ يَوْمٍ أَلْفَ حَسَنَةٍ)) فَسَأَلَهُ سَائِلٌ مِنْ جُلَسَائِهِ كَيْفَ يَكْسِبُ أَحَدُنَا أَلْفَ حَسَنَةٍ؟ قَالَ: ((يُسَبِّحُ مِائَةَ تَسْبِيحَةٍ، فَيُكتَبُ لَهُ أَلْفُ حَسَنَةٍ أَوْ يُحَطُّ عَنْهُ أَلْفُ خَطِيئَةٍ)).
adhkar.translation: (Sa’d  said: ‘We were sitting with the Messenger of Allah , and he said: ‘Are any of you unable to gain a thousand good deeds each day?’ Somebody then asked him  : How does one achieve a thousand good deeds? He replied: ‘He should say: (How perfect Allah is.) one hundred times, for a thousand good deeds are recorded for him or a thousand bad deeds are wiped away.)
adhkar.transliteration: waqaala sla allh 'lyh wslm: aaya'jizu aahadukum aan yaksiba kula yawmin aalfa hasanahin fasaaalahu saailun min julasaaihi kayfa yaksibu aahadunaa aalfa hasanahin qaala: yusabihu miaaha tasbiyhahin fayuktabu lahu aalfu hasanahin aaw yuhatu 'anhu aalfu khatiyahin.
adhkar.instruction: (Sa’d  said: ‘We were sitting with the Messenger of Allah , and he said: ‘Are any of you unable to gain a thousand good deeds each day?’ Somebody then asked him  : How does one achieve a thousand good deeds? He replied: ‘He should say: (Subhanal-lah.) one hundred times, for a thousand good deeds are recorded for him or a thousand bad deeds are wiped away.)

adhkar.arabic: ((مَنْ قَالَ: سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ الْعَظِيمِ وَبِحَمْدِهِ غُرِسَتْ لَهُ نَخْلَةٌ فِي الْجَنَّةِ)).
adhkar.translation: (Jabbir  related that the Prophet  said: ‘Whoever says: (How perfect Allah is . The Supreme, and I praise Him.) a palm tree is planted for him in paradise.)
adhkar.transliteration: man qaala: subhaana allahi al'aziymi wabihamdihi ghurisat lahu nakhlahun fiy aljanahi.
adhkar.instruction: (Jabbir  related that the Prophet  said: ‘Whoever says: (Subhanal-lahil-AAatheemi wabihamdih.) a palm tree is planted for him in paradise.)

adhkar.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((يَا عَبْدَ اللَّهِ بْنَ قَيْسٍ أَلاَ أَدُلُّكَ عَلَى كَنْزٍ مِنْ كُنُوزِ الْجَنَّةِ))؟ فَقُلْتُ: بَلَى يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، قَالَ: ((قُلْ لاَ حَوْلَ وَلاَ قُوَّةَ إِلاَّ بِاللَّهِ)).
adhkar.translation: (AAabdullah Ibn Qays  related that the Prophet  said to him: ‘ O ‘AAabdullah Ibn Qays, shall I not inform you of a treasure from the treasures of paradise?’ He  then said: ‘Say: (There is no might nor power except with Allah.))
adhkar.transliteration: waqaala sla allh 'lyh wslm: yaa 'abda allahi bna qaysin aalaa aaduluka 'alaa kanzin min kunuwzi aljanahi faqultu: balaa yaa rasuwla allahi qaala: qul laa hawla walaa quwaha iilaa biallahi.
adhkar.instruction: (AAabdullah Ibn Qays  related that the Prophet  said to him: ‘ O ‘AAabdullah Ibn Qays, shall I not inform you of a treasure from the treasures of paradise?’ He  then said: ‘Say: (La  hawla wala quwwata illa billah.))

adhkar.arabic: وَقَالَ صلى الله عليه وسلم: ((أَحَبُّ الْكَلاَمِ إِلَى اللَّهِ أَرْبَعٌ: سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ، وَالْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ، وَلاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ، وَاللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ، لاَ يَضُرُّكَ بِأَيِّهِنَّ بَدَأتَ)).
adhkar.translation: (the most beloved words to Allah are four: (How perfect Allah is, all praise is for Allah. None has the right to be worshipped except Allah and Allah is the greatest.))(it does not matter which of them you start with.)
adhkar.transliteration: waqaala sla allh 'lyh wslm: aahabu alkalaami iilaa allahi aarba'un: subhaana allahi waalhamdu lilahi walaa iilaha iilaa allahu waallahu aakbaru laa yaduruka biaayihina badaata.
adhkar.instruction: (the most beloved words to Allah are four: (Subhanal-lah, walhamdu lillah, wala ilaha illal-lah, wallahu akbar.))(it does not matter which of them you start with.)

adhkar.arabic: جَاءَ أَعْرَابِيٌّ إِلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صلى الله عليه وسلم فَقَالَ: عَلِّمْنِي كَلاماً أَقُولُهُ: قَالَ: ((قُلْ: لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لاَ شَرِيكَ لَهُ، اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ كَبِيراً، وَالْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ كَثِيراً، سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ رَبِّ العَالَمِينَ، لاَ حَوْلَ وَلاَ قُوَّةَ إِلاَّ بِاللَّهِ الْعَزِيزِ الْحَكِيمِ)) قَالَ: فَهَؤُلاَءِ لِرَبِّي، فَمَا لِي؟ قَالَ: ((قُلْ: اللَّهُمَّ اغْفِرْ لِي، وَارْحَمْنِي، وَاهْدِنِي، وَارْزُقْنِي)).
adhkar.translation: (SaAAd Ibn Abee Waqqas  narrated that a man came to the Messenger of Allah  and said to him: ‘Teach me something which I should say?’ He said: ‘Say: (None has the right to be worshipped except Allah, alone without partener. Allah is most great and much praise is for Allah. How perfect Allah is, Lord of the worlds. There is no might nor power except with Allah, The Exalted in might, The Wise.) the man then said: ‘These are for my Lord, and what is for me?’ He  replied: ‘Say:(O Allah, forgive me, have mercy upon me, guide me and grant me sustenance.))
adhkar.transliteration: jaa'a aa'raabiyun iilaa rasuwli allahi sla allh 'lyh wslm faqaala: 'alimniy kalamaan aaquwluhu: qaala: qul: laa iilaha iilaa allahu wahdahu laa shariyka lahu allahu aakbaru kabiyraan waalhamdu lilahi kathiyraan subhaana allahi rabi al'aalamiyna laa hawla walaa quwaha iilaa biall
adhkar.instruction: (SaAAd Ibn Abee Waqqas  narrated that a man came to the Messenger of Allah  and said to him: ‘Teach me something which I should say?’ He said: ‘Say: (La ilaha illal-lah, wahdahu la shareeka lah, Allahu akbaru kabeera, walhamdu lillahi katheera, subhanal-lahi rabbil-AAalameen, la hawla wala quwwata illa billahil-AAazeezil-hakeem.) the man then said: ‘These are for my Lord, and what is for me?’ He  replied: ‘Say:(Allahummagh-fir lee, warhamnee, wahdinee, warzuqnee.))

adhkar.arabic: كَانَ الرَّجُلُ إِذَا أَسْلَمَ عَلَّمَهُ النَّبيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم الصَّلاَةَ ثُمَّ أَمَرَهُ أَنْ يَدْعُوَ بِهَؤُلاَءِ الْكَلِمَاتِ: ((اللَّهُمَّ اغْفِرِ لِي، وَارْحَمْنِي، وَاهْدِنِي، وَعَافِنِي وَارْزُقْنِي)).
adhkar.translation: (Tariq Al-AshjaAAee  said: ‘When someone would embrace Islam, the Prophet  would teach him how to perform prayer and then order him to supplicate with the following words: (O Allah, forgive me, have mercy upon me, guide me, give me health and grant me sustenance.))
adhkar.transliteration: kaana alrajulu iidhaa aaslama 'alamahu alnabyu sla allh 'lyh wslm alsalaaha thuma aamarahu aan yad'uwa bihaulaa'i alkalimaati: allahuma aghfiri liy waarhamniy waahdiniy wa'aafiniy waarzuqniy.
adhkar.instruction: (Tariq Al-AshjaAAee  said: ‘When someone would embrace Islam, the Prophet  would teach him how to perform prayer and then order him to supplicate with the following words: (Allahummagh-fir lee, warhamnee, wahdinee, waAAafinee warzuqnee.))

adhkar.arabic: ((إِنَّ أَفْضَلَ الدُّعَاءِ الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ، وَأَفْضَلَ الذِّكْرِ لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ)).
adhkar.translation: (Jabir  Ibn ‘AAabdullah  related that the Messenger of Allah  said: ‘Verily, the best supplication is: (All praise is for Allah.) and indeed, the best form of remembrance is: (None has the right to be worshipped except Allah.))
adhkar.transliteration: iina aafdala aldu'aa'i alhamdu lilahi waaafdala aldhikri laa iilaha iilaa allahu.
adhkar.instruction: (Jabir  Ibn ‘AAabdullah  related that the Messenger of Allah  said: ‘Verily, the best supplication is: (Alhamdu lillah) and indeed, the best form of remembrance is: (La ilaha illal-lah.))

adhkar.arabic: الباقيات الصالحات : سبحان الله والحمد لله ، ولا إله إلا الله ،والله أكبر ،و لا حول ولا قوة إلا بالله
adhkar.translation: The good deeds which endure are 'Glorified is Allah', and 'The praise is for Allah', and 'but There is none worthy of worship Allah', and 'Allah is the Most Great', and 'There is no power and no might except by Allah'
adhkar.transliteration: albaqyat alsalhat : sbhan allh walhmd llh wla ilh ila allh wallh akbr w la hwl wla qwh ila ballh
adhkar.instruction: The good deeds which endure are 'Subhaanallaah.', and 'Walhamdulillaah.', and 'Wa laa ilaaha illallaah'., and 'Wallaahu Akbar',and 'Wa laa hawla wa laa guwawala illaa billaah'.

adhkar.arabic: عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو رضي الله عنه قَالَ: ((رَأَيْتُ النَّبيَّ صلى الله عليه وسلم يَعْقِدُ التَّسْبِيحَ)) وفي زيادةٍ: ((بِيَمِينِهِ)).
adhkar.translation: (Tasbeeh, it means here, to say: (Subhanal-lah, alhamdu lillah, Allahu akbar.)) (AAabdullah Ibn AAamr  said: ’I saw the prophet  make tasbeeh with his right hand.)
adhkar.transliteration: 'an 'abdi allahi bni 'amrinw rdy allh 'nh qaala: raaaytu alnabya sla allh 'lyh wslm ya'qidu altasbiyha wfy zyadhin: biyamiynihi.

adhkar.category_name: Types of goodness and good etiquette for community life
adhkar.translation: When evening descends, bring your children indoors for the devils scatter out at this hour. Then after the passing of an hour (i.e. the first hour) of the night, (you may) let them (the children) go. Close your doors while mentioning the Name of Allah, for the devil may not open a closed door. Fasten your waterskins mentioning the Name of Allah. Cover your eating vessels mentioning the Name of Allah even if you just put something over it, and extinguish your lamps. Peace and blessing be upon our Prophet, Muhammad, and upon his family and his Companions, all of them.

adhkar.arabic: رَبَّنَا تَقَبَّلْ مِنَّا إِنَّكَ أَنْتَ السَّمِيعُ الْعَلِيمُ
adhkar.translation: Our Lord, accept [this] from us. Indeed, You are the Hearing, the Knowing.
adhkar.transliteration: Rabbana taqabbal minna innaka antas-Samee'ul-'Aleem
adhkar.instruction: Surah 2, Ayah 127 (2:127)

adhkar.arabic: رَبَّنَا وَاجْعَلْنَا مُسْلِمَيْنِ لَكَ وَمِنْ ذُرِّيَّتِنَا أُمَّةً مُسْلِمَةً لَكَ وَأَرِنَا مَنَاسِكَنَا وَتُبْ عَلَيْنَا إِنَّكَ أَنْتَ التَّوَّابُ الرَّحِيمُ
adhkar.translation: Our Lord, and make us Muslims [in submission] to You and from our descendants a Muslim nation [in submission] to You. And show us our rites and accept our repentance. Indeed, You are the Accepting of repentance, the Merciful.
adhkar.transliteration: Rabbana waj'alna Muslimayni laka wa min dhurriyyatina ummatan Muslimatan laka wa arina manasikana wa tub 'alayna innaka antat-Tawwabur-Raheem
adhkar.instruction: Surah 2, Ayah 128 (2:128)

adhkar.arabic: رَبَّنَا آتِنَا فِي الدُّنْيَا حَسَنَةً وَفِي الْآخِرَةِ حَسَنَةً وَقِنَا عَذَابَ النَّارِ
adhkar.translation: Our Lord, give us in this world [that which is] good and in the Hereafter [that which is] good and protect us from the punishment of the Fire.
adhkar.transliteration: Rabbana aatina fid-dunya hasanatan wa fil-aakhirati hasanatan wa qina 'adhaban-naar
adhkar.instruction: Surah 2, Ayah 201 (2:201)

adhkar.arabic: رَبَّنَا أَفْرِغْ عَلَيْنَا صَبْرًا وَثَبِّتْ أَقْدَامَنَا وَانْصُرْنَا عَلَى الْقَوْمِ الْكَافِرِينَ
adhkar.translation: Our Lord, pour upon us patience and plant firmly our feet and give us victory over the disbelieving people.
adhkar.transliteration: Rabbana afrigh 'alayna sabran wa thabbit aqdaamana wansurna 'alal-qawmil-kaafireen
adhkar.instruction: Surah 2, Ayah 250 (2:250)

adhkar.arabic: سَمِعْنَا وَأَطَعْنَا غُفْرَانَكَ رَبَّنَا وَإِلَيْكَ الْمَصِيرُ
adhkar.translation: We hear and we obey. [We seek] Your forgiveness, our Lord, and to You is the [final] destination.
adhkar.transliteration: Sami'na wa ata'na ghufranaka Rabbana wa ilaykal-maseer
adhkar.instruction: Surah 2, Ayah 285 (2:285)

adhkar.arabic: رَبَّنَا لَا تُؤَاخِذْنَا إِنْ نَسِينَا أَوْ أَخْطَأْنَا
adhkar.translation: Our Lord, do not impose blame upon us if we have forgotten or erred.
adhkar.transliteration: Rabbana la tu'aakhidhna in naseena aw akhta'na
adhkar.instruction: Surah 2, Ayah 286 (2:286)

adhkar.arabic: رَبَّنَا وَلَا تَحْمِلْ عَلَيْنَا إِصْرًا كَمَا حَمَلْتَهُ عَلَى الَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِنَا
adhkar.translation: Our Lord, and lay not upon us a burden like that which You laid upon those before us.
adhkar.transliteration: Rabbana wa la tahmil 'alayna isran kama hamaltahu 'alal-ladheena min qablina
adhkar.instruction: Surah 2, Ayah 286 (2:286)

adhkar.arabic: رَبَّنَا وَلَا تُحَمِّلْنَا مَا لَا طَاقَةَ لَنَا بِهِ وَاعْفُ عَنَّا وَاغْفِرْ لَنَا وَارْحَمْنَا أَنْتَ مَوْلَانَا فَانْصُرْنَا عَلَى الْقَوْمِ الْكَافِرِينَ
adhkar.translation: Our Lord, and burden us not with that which we have no ability to bear. And pardon us; and forgive us; and have mercy upon us. You are our protector, so give us victory over the disbelieving people.
adhkar.transliteration: Rabbana wa la tuhammilna ma la taqata lana bihi wa'fu 'anna waghfir lana warhamna anta mawlana fansurna 'alal-qawmil-kaafireen
adhkar.instruction: Surah 2, Ayah 286 (2:286)

adhkar.arabic: رَبَّنَا لَا تُزِغْ قُلُوبَنَا بَعْدَ إِذْ هَدَيْتَنَا وَهَبْ لَنَا مِنْ لَدُنْكَ رَحْمَةً إِنَّكَ أَنْتَ الْوَهَّابُ
adhkar.translation: Our Lord, let not our hearts deviate after You have guided us and grant us from Yourself mercy. Indeed, You are the Bestower.
adhkar.transliteration: Rabbana la tuzigh quloobana ba'da idh hadaytana wa hab lana min ladunka rahmatan innaka antal-Wahhaab
adhkar.instruction: Surah 3, Ayah 8 (3:8)

adhkar.arabic: رَبَّنَا إِنَّكَ جَامِعُ النَّاسِ لِيَوْمٍ لَا رَيْبَ فِيهِ إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يُخْلِفُ الْمِيعَادَ
adhkar.translation: Our Lord, surely You will gather the people for a Day about which there is no doubt. Indeed, Allah does not fail in His promise.
adhkar.transliteration: Rabbana innaka jaami'un-naasi li-yawmin la rayba feehi innallaha la yukhliful-mee'aad
adhkar.instruction: Surah 3, Ayah 9 (3:9)

adhkar.arabic: رَبَّنَا إِنَّنَا آمَنَّا فَاغْفِرْ لَنَا ذُنُوبَنَا وَقِنَا عَذَابَ النَّارِ
adhkar.translation: Our Lord, indeed we have believed, so forgive us our sins and protect us from the punishment of the Fire.
adhkar.transliteration: Rabbana innana aamanna faghfir lana dhunoobana wa qina 'adhaban-naar
adhkar.instruction: Surah 3, Ayah 16 (3:16)

adhkar.arabic: رَبَّنَا آمَنَّا بِمَا أَنْزَلْتَ وَاتَّبَعْنَا الرَّسُولَ فَاكْتُبْنَا مَعَ الشَّاهِدِينَ
adhkar.translation: Our Lord, we have believed in what You revealed and have followed the messenger, so register us among the witnesses.
adhkar.transliteration: Rabbana aamanna bima anzalta wattaba'nar-rasoola faktubna ma'ash-shaahideen
adhkar.instruction: Surah 3, Ayah 53 (3:53)

adhkar.arabic: رَبَّنَا اغْفِرْ لَنَا ذُنُوبَنَا وَإِسْرَافَنَا فِي أَمْرِنَا وَثَبِّتْ أَقْدَامَنَا وَانْصُرْنَا عَلَى الْقَوْمِ الْكَافِرِينَ
adhkar.translation: Our Lord, forgive us our sins and the excess [committed] in our affairs and plant firmly our feet and give us victory over the disbelieving people.
adhkar.transliteration: Rabbana ighfir lana dhunoobana wa israafana fee amrina wa thabbit aqdaamana wansurna 'alal-qawmil-kaafireen
adhkar.instruction: Surah 3, Ayah 147 (3:147)

adhkar.arabic: رَبَّنَا مَا خَلَقْتَ هَذَا بَاطِلًا سُبْحَانَكَ فَقِنَا عَذَابَ النَّارِ
adhkar.translation: Our Lord, You did not create this aimlessly; exalted are You [above such a thing]; then protect us from the punishment of the Fire.
adhkar.transliteration: Rabbana ma khalaqta hadha baatilan subhaanaka faqina 'adhaban-naar
adhkar.instruction: Surah 3, Ayah 191 (3:191)

adhkar.arabic: رَبَّنَا إِنَّكَ مَنْ تُدْخِلِ النَّارَ فَقَدْ أَخْزَيْتَهُ وَمَا لِلظَّالِمِينَ مِنْ أَنْصَارٍ
adhkar.translation: Our Lord, indeed whoever You admit to the Fire - You have disgraced him, and for the wrongdoers there are no helpers.
adhkar.transliteration: Rabbana innaka man tudkhilin-naara faqad akhzaytahu wa ma lidh-dhaalimeena min ansaar
adhkar.instruction: Surah 3, Ayah 192 (3:192)

adhkar.arabic: رَبَّنَا إِنَّنَا سَمِعْنَا مُنَادِيًا يُنَادِي لِلْإِيمَانِ أَنْ آمِنُوا بِرَبِّكُمْ فَآمَنَّا رَبَّنَا فَاغْفِرْ لَنَا ذُنُوبَنَا وَكَفِّرْ عَنَّا سَيِّئَاتِنَا وَتَوَفَّنَا مَعَ الْأَبْرَارِ
adhkar.translation: Our Lord, indeed we have heard a caller calling to faith, [saying], 'Believe in your Lord,' and we have believed. Our Lord, so forgive us our sins and remove from us our misdeeds and cause us to die with the righteous.
adhkar.transliteration: Rabbana innana sami'na munadiyan yunaadee lil-eemani an aaminoo bi-Rabbikum fa-aamanna Rabbana faghfir lana dhunoobana wa kaffir 'anna sayyi'aatina wa tawaffana ma'al-abraar
adhkar.instruction: Surah 3, Ayah 193 (3:193)

adhkar.arabic: رَبَّنَا وَآتِنَا مَا وَعَدْتَنَا عَلَى رُسُلِكَ وَلَا تُخْزِنَا يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ إِنَّكَ لَا تُخْلِفُ الْمِيعَادَ
adhkar.translation: Our Lord, and grant us what You promised us through Your messengers and do not disgrace us on the Day of Resurrection. Indeed, You do not fail in [Your] promise.
adhkar.transliteration: Rabbana wa aatina ma wa'adtana 'ala rusulika wa la tukhzina yawmal-qiyaamati innaka la tukhliful-mee'aad
adhkar.instruction: Surah 3, Ayah 194 (3:194)

adhkar.arabic: رَبَّنَا آمَنَّا فَاكْتُبْنَا مَعَ الشَّاهِدِينَ
adhkar.translation: Our Lord, we have believed, so register us among the witnesses.
adhkar.transliteration: Rabbana aamanna faktubna ma'ash-shaahideen
adhkar.instruction: Surah 5, Ayah 83 (5:83)

adhkar.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ رَبَّنَا أَنْزِلْ عَلَيْنَا مَائِدَةً مِنَ السَّمَاءِ تَكُونُ لَنَا عِيدًا لِأَوَّلِنَا وَآخِرِنَا وَآيَةً مِنْكَ وَارْزُقْنَا وَأَنْتَ خَيْرُ الرَّازِقِينَ
adhkar.translation: O Allah, our Lord, send down to us a table [spread with food] from the heaven to be for us a festival for the first of us and the last of us and a sign from You. And provide for us, and You are the best of providers.
adhkar.transliteration: Allahumma Rabbana anzil 'alayna maa'idatan minas-samaa'i takoonu lana 'eedan li-awwalina wa aakhirina wa aayatan minka warzuqna wa anta khayrur-raaziqeen
adhkar.instruction: Surah 5, Ayah 114 (5:114)

adhkar.arabic: رَبَّنَا ظَلَمْنَا أَنْفُسَنَا وَإِنْ لَمْ تَغْفِرْ لَنَا وَتَرْحَمْنَا لَنَكُونَنَّ مِنَ الْخَاسِرِينَ
adhkar.translation: Our Lord, we have wronged ourselves, and if You do not forgive us and have mercy upon us, we will surely be among the losers.
adhkar.transliteration: Rabbana dhalamna anfusana wa in lam taghfir lana wa tarhamna lanakoonanna minal-khaasireen
adhkar.instruction: Surah 7, Ayah 23 (7:23)

adhkar.arabic: رَبَّنَا لَا تَجْعَلْنَا مَعَ الْقَوْمِ الظَّالِمِينَ
adhkar.translation: Our Lord, do not place us with the wrongdoing people.
adhkar.transliteration: Rabbana la taj'alna ma'al-qawmidh-dhaalimeen
adhkar.instruction: Surah 7, Ayah 47 (7:47)

adhkar.arabic: رَبَّنَا افْتَحْ بَيْنَنَا وَبَيْنَ قَوْمِنَا بِالْحَقِّ وَأَنْتَ خَيْرُ الْفَاتِحِينَ
adhkar.translation: Our Lord, decide between us and our people in truth, and You are the best of those who give decision.
adhkar.transliteration: Rabbana iftah baynana wa bayna qawmina bil-haqqi wa anta khayrul-faatiheen
adhkar.instruction: Surah 7, Ayah 89 (7:89)

adhkar.arabic: رَبَّنَا أَفْرِغْ عَلَيْنَا صَبْرًا وَتَوَفَّنَا مُسْلِمِينَ
